summary: Backend-only test run for Nova AI Assistant API. 9/9 pytest tests passed against the external EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Verified root info (model=amazon.nova-lite-v1:0), session create/list/delete (sorted desc by updated_at), chat via Bedrock Nova Lite Converse REST API with multi-turn context (assistant correctly recalled the name 'Pyro' across turns), message persistence + chronological order, on-the-fly session creation for unknown session_id, empty-message 400, no MongoDB _id leakage in any response, and Whisper-1 transcription via Emergent LLM proxy with a generated tiny WAV (returned 200 with text field). Fixed one small integration bug: /api/transcribe was passing a string path to OpenAISpeechToText, which litellm rejected (it expects bytes / io.IOBase / PathLike / tuple). Changed to pass an opened binary file handle; transcription now returns 200.

minor.issue: GET on a non-existent session returns 200 with []. Acceptable but consider 404 for clarity (not blocking).
Returns 500 with raw exception string on failure. Consider mapping to 502 when upstream Whisper proxy fails (not blocking).

logger is defined AFTER it is referenced inside call_bedrock_nova/transcribe_audio. It still works because functions resolve names at call time and logging.basicConfig runs at import, but it is fragile. Move logger init above the routes.

action_items: Optional: relocate `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` above the route handlers in /app/backend/server.py (currently defined at the bottom).
Optional: have /api/transcribe return 502 (instead of 500) when the upstream STT provider fails, similar to the Bedrock error handling.
No further action required for the reported features — backend is fully working end-to-end (including multi-turn Nova Lite context and Whisper transcription).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is well-structured (<250 lines), uses async motor, excludes _id via projections everywhere, uses uuid string IDs (good for JSON), and reads all secrets from env. No hardcoded URLs/credentials.
Bedrock Converse REST call shape and parsing are correct; system prompt is sent via the dedicated 'system' field.

Transcription endpoint previously failed because the underlying emergentintegrations.OpenAISpeechToText forwards `file` directly to litellm.atranscription, which requires bytes/IOBase/PathLike. Fixed by opening the temp file in binary mode before passing it in.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (transcribe_audio now passes an opened binary file handle to stt.transcribe)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed sessions and clean them up in teardown_class. A 1-second 16kHz mono WAV is generated in pytest tmp_path for transcription.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_nova_api.py with shared base_url/api_client fixtures in conftest.py. Tests hit the external EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. The Bedrock chat tests rely on the real Nova Lite endpoint and the multi-turn test asserts the model echoes 'pyro' — if the model becomes flaky, soften that assertion. Transcription uses a generated silent-ish WAV; Whisper may return an empty string for very short audio, which is allowed by the test.
rca_of_issue: Bug: POST /api/transcribe returned 500 with 'Expected entry at `file` to be bytes, an io.IOBase instance, PathLike or a tuple but received <class \'str\'> instead.' Reproduction: any multipart upload to /api/transcribe. Root cause: server.py called `stt.transcribe(file=tmp_path, ...)` where tmp_path is a `str`. emergentintegrations.OpenAISpeechToText.transcribe passes `file` straight through to litellm.atranscription, and the underlying OpenAI Python client rejects plain strings. Mitigation: open the temp file in binary mode (`open(tmp_path, 'rb')`) and pass the resulting file handle. Verified working — /api/transcribe now returns 200 with a `text` field.
